title: bothriocline angolensis
intro: Bothriocline angolensis, also known as Angola bush or Blue Hill, is a flowering plant species in the family Lamiaceae. It is a shrub-like plant with blue flowers and has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The plant is said to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing fever and treating skin infections. The herbal preparations of Bothriocline angolensis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions.
